title: trientalis europaea subsp. latifolia
intro: Trientalis europaea subsp. latifolia, also known as the broad-leaved waxy bell, is a subspecies of the Trientalis europaea plant, a perennial herb native to Europe and Asia. The plant has been used for its medicinal properties, including its antispasmodic and anti-inflammatory effects, which can help alleviate digestive issues and reduce swelling. The health benefits of Trientalis europaea subsp. latifolia include its ability to stimulate digestion, calm the nervous system, and support the immune system. Herbal preparations of the plant, such as infusions and tinctures, can be used to harness its medicinal properties and promote overall well-being.
